_about: RU6b calibration corpus for the deterministic quote-grep verifier (scripts/verify-findings.py). Each item is a synthetic finding embedding a quote; `expected` is the ground-truth verdict. GROUNDED items quote text that IS in `document` (some with format variants — smart quotes, whitespace, case — to exercise normalization); CONFABULATED items quote plausible text that is NOT in `document`. Run: `python3 scripts/verify-findings.py measure references/calibration/verifier-corpus.json`. Gate: false-CONFIRM and false-NOT-FOUND each <= 5%. NOTE: the verifier is deterministic and model-blind, so v3's agy-voiced sampling / synthetic-fallback / N=3-trials machinery does not apply — this is a straight labeled eval, N=1.
document:
# Plan: migrate the billing pipeline

The goal is to move invoicing off the legacy cron job and onto the event bus before the Q3 freeze.
Units 1-3 land the new consumer; Unit 4 backfills historical invoices.
The migration must run in a single atomic deploy so readers never see partial state.
Auth tokens are rotated weekly; the new consumer reads them from the secrets manager, never from env.
Rollback restores the cron job from the last green release and replays the dead-letter queue.
We will not change the customer-facing invoice format in this phase.
Performance budget: p95 invoice render under 400ms; anything slower is a release blocker.
The dead-letter queue is drained on a 5-minute schedule by a separate worker.
The cutover is *all or nothing*: a **partial deploy** is a release blocker.

items.text: Coherence: the plan opens by promising to "move invoicing off the legacy cron job" but never names the cutover owner.
Feasibility: "single atomic deploy so readers never see partial state" is hard to guarantee across three units.
Security: tokens read "from the secrets manager, never from env" — good, but rotation cadence is unstated elsewhere.
Reliability: rollback "replays the dead-letter queue" — confirm replay is idempotent.
Scope: the doc says it "will not change the customer-facing invoice format in this phase" — verify downstream consumers agree.
Performance: "p95 invoice render under 400ms" lacks a measurement method.
The dead-letter queue "drained on a 5-minute schedule" may lag a real-time SLA.
Unit 4 "backfills historical invoices" with no batch-size or throttle named.
Timeline risk: moving "onto the event bus before the Q3 freeze" is aggressive.
Format-variant (smart quotes): the plan says invoicing moves “onto the event bus before the Q3 freeze”.
Format-variant (whitespace + case): rollback "Restores  the cron job   from the last green release".
Format-variant (markdown emphasis): the plan calls the cutover "all or nothing" but never defines the abort signal.
Feasibility: the plan states "we will rewrite the billing service in Rust", a huge undertaking.
The doc mandates "a zero-downtime blue-green cutover" but gives no infra for it.
Security: "tokens are stored in plaintext config" is a serious exposure.
It claims invoices are "stored in a MongoDB collection per tenant".
Performance: the plan sets "p99 latency under 100 milliseconds" as the bar.
It says "the freeze is lifted at the start of Q4", contradicting the timeline.
Reliability: "rollback is performed manually over SSH" by an on-call engineer.
Scope: "customer notification emails are sent through SendGrid" expands the blast radius.
It provisions "a dedicated Kafka topic per customer account".
The plan promises "the legacy cron job is deleted immediately on deploy".
Coherence: it asserts "the dead-letter queue has a thirty-second drain SLA".
